description: Audit and correct a three-sheet financial model with cascading errors across Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow Statement, then document all findings on an Audit_Findings sheet.

natural_language.prompt:
Open the financial model spreadsheet at /home/ga/Documents/financial_model.xlsx. It contains three sheets — Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow Statement — for a small retail business's Q4 2024 results. The model was prepared by a junior analyst and contains multiple errors causing the financial statements to be internally inconsistent.

Audit and fix all errors so that: (1) Revenue minus all costs and taxes equals Net Income on the Income Statement, (2) Total Assets equals Total Liabilities plus Total Equity on the Balance Sheet, (3) Net Cash Change on the Cash Flow Statement equals Ending Cash minus Beginning Cash, and (4) Net Income is identical on both the Income Statement and Cash Flow Statement. Fix only formulas and references — do not change any raw input data values.

After fixing all errors, create an Audit_Findings sheet documenting each error found: the sheet name, cell reference, what was wrong, and the correction applied. Include cross-check formulas verifying that the Balance Sheet balances and that Net Income ties across statements. Calculate the total dollar impact of the errors on reported Net Income.

Save the completed workbook as /home/ga/Documents/corrected_financial_model.xlsx.
